title: Write a Git pre-commit Hook
description:
## Automate a Local Gate

Git hooks let you run checks automatically around git operations. They live in **`.git/hooks/`** and must be **executable**.

A repository is ready at **`/root/hookrepo`**.

**Your task:** create an **executable** `pre-commit` hook at **`/root/hookrepo/.git/hooks/pre-commit`** that prints exactly **`pre-commit ran`** when executed.

> 💡 The file needs a shebang (`#!/bin/bash`) and the execute bit (`chmod +x`). Git ignores hooks that aren't executable.

hints.title: Create and arm the hook
hints.body: Write the shebang and echo, then make the file executable so Git (and the validator) can run it.

validation.description: Checks that the pre-commit hook exists, is executable, and prints the expected line.
